definition_plain: A large language model (LLM) is a neural network trained on massive amounts of text that can generate, summarise, translate, and reason about language. It is the foundational engine underneath nearly every AI product — the thing that everything else (agents, RAG systems, guardrails) is built around. LLMs predict the most likely next token, and from that simple mechanism emerges surprisingly general capability.
source_label: OpenAI et al., 2023 — GPT-4 Technical Report
